title: Chrysurus aureus
intro:
The Chrysanthemum flower, specifically Chrysanthemum parthenium (also known as Chrysanthemum coccineum or Chrysanthemum indicum), is a flowering plant in the Asteraceae family, commonly known as Chrysanthemum or Chrysurus aureus is not a commonly known plant name.  Chrysanthemum parthenium, however, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving sleep quality, and possessing antimicrobial properties.

The bioactive constituents of Chrysanthemum parthenium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These constituents have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects, making Chrysanthemum parthenium a valuable plant for various health applications.

The most common herbal preparations made with Chrysanthemum parthenium include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, headache, and digestive issues. Additionally, Chrysanthemum parthenium is also used as an ingredient in traditional Chinese medicine to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
